Matt's Script Archive - Awesome Web Server Scripts

Did you know...? Like many great stories, Dragonball starts out faultless. But, as the story progresses, the writers often forget about the small yet significant points they stated earlier. Some attempted "cover-ups" of earlier errors can also led to more errors. Dragonball Z tells of a Super Saiyan, who appears every 1000 years. Gokou is shown to be this Saiyan, so he is supposed to be the only one able to reach Super Saian level. Unexplained: Later, Trunks, Vegeta, and Gohan are also able to reach Super Saiyan mode To try to clarify this error, Movie 8 introduces Brolly, the Legendary Saiyan who apears every 1000 years. Unexplained: It is shown that Gokou can indeed reach SSJ2 and SSJ3 mode, and that Gohan can reach SSJ2 mode too. To try to cover up this error, another term is introduced- the legendary Saiyan who appears every 1000 years- and that is Gokou when he reaches Super Gokou 4 mode. When Cell exploded, he regenerated from a single surviving brain cell. Unexplained: Cell's entire upper body (including the head and thus the brain) was blown away in an episode, but he still was able regenerate (without a brain cell). In the Boo Series, Gohan trained to become Mystic Gohan, a stage where he was able to become stronger than reaching Super Saiyan form. Therefore, he does not reach SSJ mode when he fights Boo. Unexplained: Gohan reaches SSJ form once again in Dragonball GT.
